what_it_does: Defines the ViMark React component: Vi's diamond avatar/brand mark. Renders an SVG diamond (polygon) with a white fill, a gold (#E0C010) stroke, and a horizontal-line pattern fill; when `animated` is true the pattern lines animate opacity (the 'thinking' line-fill). Optionally overlays the 'Vi' glyph (with a smaller subscript 'i'). Sizable via `size`. Exposed on window.
observations: Props: size (default 32), animated (default false), showGlyph (default true).
Generates a per-instance random SVG pattern id (`vi-pat-` + random base36) to avoid <defs> id collisions when multiple marks render.
Pattern lines use opacity 0.55 when animated else 0.35; when animated an <animate> element pulses opacity 0.2->0.7->0.2 over 1.6s indefinitely.
Diamond drawn as polygon points '50,8 92,50 50,92 8,50' on a 0 0 100 100 viewBox, twice — once white-filled with gold stroke, once filled with the line pattern.
Hardcodes the gold colour #E0C010 directly in the SVG (stroke + pattern line) rather than using a CSS token.
fontSize for the overlaid glyph is set to size * 0.42; the 'i' is rendered at 0.62em.
Assigns window.ViMark (browser-global pattern, global React, no imports/exports).
standouts: The animated line-fill pattern is the visual signal for 'Vi is thinking' — motion encodes agent activity in the brand mark itself.
Per-instance random pattern id is a deliberate guard so multiple marks on one page don't share/clobber an SVG defs id.
conventions: Browser-global component pattern (window.ViMark, global React), consistent with the rest of the Vi kit.
Breaks the design-system token discipline by hardcoding the gold #E0C010 in the SVG (vi.css/README treat gold as --accent-gold / a brand token elsewhere).
concerns: Math.random() pattern id is regenerated on every render, so the id changes each render (works, but not memoised — minor churn).
Hardcoded #E0C010 means a palette/token change to the brand gold would not propagate to this SVG mark.
notes: Vi's diamond brand mark as a sizable SVG with an optional pulsing line-fill ('thinking') and an optional 'Vi' glyph; gold is hardcoded inline rather than tokenised.
questions: Should the gold be sourced from the --accent-gold token (used elsewhere) rather than the inline #E0C010 literal?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comment ('the Vi diamond avatar') matches the diamond SVG mark with optional animation and glyph it renders.
apparent_themes: Brand identity as a reusable component (one mark, many sizes/contexts)
intention_signals: Tries to provide a single reusable Vi avatar that can stand in for Vi anywhere it 'speaks' and visibly indicate when Vi is actively working via the animated fill.
novelty: Encoding 'thinking' as an animated line-fill inside a diamond brand mark, with a per-instance pattern id to keep many instances independent, is a distinctive touch.
contribution: Contributes the canonical Vi avatar component — the diamond mark with its thinking-animation and optional wordmark — reused across the kit (e.g. inside the status pill and chat avatars).
summary_for_embedding: React ViMark component rendering Vi's diamond brand avatar as a gold-stroked SVG with a line-fill pattern that pulses when animated ('thinking') and an optional 'Vi' glyph, sizable and exposed on window.
intention_for_embedding: To give Vi a single reusable avatar mark that signals identity everywhere it speaks and indicates active thinking via animated fill.
